thesis: Vertically integrated Earth observation company designing, manufacturing, launching, and operating its own satellites. 20+ satellites in orbit with AI-first constellation (Merlin) launching October 2026. FY2025 revenue $17.7M (+38% YoY) with exceptional 72% gross margins. $130M cash runway post-January 2026 raise. Unique sovereign partnership model selling satellites + technology transfer to nations (Portugal, Malaysia, Albania, Australia). Merlin constellation is make-or-break catalyst.

drivers: AI-first, 1-meter daily remapping drives defense customer adoption
Sovereign program replication — 3-5 similar deals annually
Acquisition by Maxar, Planet, or defense prime at $800M-$1.2B

risks: Merlin launch failure or technical delays
Competitive pressure from Planet Labs and Maxar
Cash burn forces additional dilutive financing

base_case.assumptions: Merlin launches successfully late 2026; 2-3 additional sovereign deals signed; Revenue grows to $35-40M by 2027; EBITDA losses narrow to $5-10M; Operational break-even by 2028.

catalysts.event: CEiiA Satellite Delivery — $18M Portugal contract validation
Merlin Launch — Make-or-break moment for AI-first constellation
New Sovereign Partnerships — Malaysia, Albania expansions

key_risks: Customer concentration — top 2 customers ~60% of backlog
Binary Merlin launch catalyst (Oct 2026) — failure could destroy credibility
Small-cap with limited liquidity and ~25% dilution potential
Competition from Planet Labs' 200+ satellite constellation
Pre-revenue scale with high execution risk
